audit_items.proof_trace: All rax_free call-sites in rax.c follow a strict discipline: required values (next-child pointer, key data) are extracted before freeing, and the local variable is either immediately reassigned or the function returns. The single instance where a freed pointer value is used after rax_free — raxRemoveChild(h, child) at L1062 after child is freed at L1052 — only compares the freed pointer's address against stored child pointers in the parent; it never dereferences child. Redis's single-threaded execution model ensures no allocator reuse can occur in the narrow window between L1052 and L1062, making this technically-UB comparison safe in practice. No double-free paths exist.
